options.info: PCSX2 graphics backend. Auto picks Metal on macOS. Software runs on CPU only (much slower; useful for debugging rendering bugs or for games with hardware-renderer regressions).
options.label: GS Renderer

options.info: Run the VU1 microprogram on its own thread instead of the EE thread. Compatible with the vast majority of games; significantly reduces EE-thread saturation on Apple Silicon's interpreter-only path. Disable only if a specific game shows MTVU-related glitches.
options.label: Multi-Threaded VU1

options.info: Skip the PS2 BIOS Sony intro and region-check screen on launch. Disable if you want to see the BIOS screen (e.g. to verify your BIOS region or to use the BIOS browser).
options.label: Fast Boot

options.info: Target emulation speed during normal gameplay (relative to PS2's native rate). 100% is real-time. Lower for handheld-style throttling, higher for fast-forwarding through cutscenes.
options.label: Normal Speed

options.info: Target speed when fast-forward is engaged. The frontend's fast-forward toggle scales emulation by this factor.
options.label: Fast-Forward Speed

options.info: Target speed when slow-motion is engaged. The frontend's slow-motion toggle scales emulation by this factor.
options.label: Slow-Motion Speed

options.info: Underclocks or overclocks the emulated Emotion Engine CPU. Negative values reduce work-per-cycle (compatibility/perf tradeoff); positive values speed CPU-bound games at the cost of timing accuracy. Most games should stay at 100%.
options.label: EE Cycle Rate

options.info: Makes the emulated Emotion Engine skip cycles. Stronger underclock than EE Cycle Rate; can recover frame-rate in slow scenes at the cost of visible glitches. Leave Disabled unless a specific game needs it.
options.label: EE Cycle Skipping

options.info: Pin emulation threads to specific CPU cores. Can reduce stutter on systems with heterogeneous cores (e.g. Apple Silicon performance/efficiency split). Default off.
options.label: Thread Pinning

options.info: Load pnach cheat files from the cheats/ resource directory on game launch. Off by default; the in-game OSD logs each loaded patch line.
options.label: Enable Cheats

options.info: Allow the emulated PS2 to read files from the host filesystem (homebrew-only feature; retail games never use it). Off by default.
options.label: Enable Host Filesystem

options.info: Load the entire disc image into RAM before booting. Eliminates in-game disc-read stutter at the cost of extra memory usage and a slower initial boot. Off by default.
options.label: CDVD Precache

options.info: When Fast Boot is enabled, also auto-fast-forward the brief BIOS boot animation. Has no effect when Fast Boot is disabled.
options.label: Fast-Forward Through BIOS

options.info: Frames the GS can queue before the EE must wait. Lower values reduce input latency at the cost of frame-pacing smoothness. 0 is the lowest-latency 'optimal' mode (re-paces every frame).
options.label: Maximum Frame Latency

options.info: Adjust emulation speed slightly to align with the host display's refresh rate. Reduces audio drift on non-60Hz monitors. May be cosmetic in libretro mode — the frontend owns presentation timing.
options.label: Sync to Host Refresh Rate

options.info: Synchronize frame submission with the host display's vblank. May be cosmetic in libretro mode — the frontend owns presentation.
options.label: Vertical Sync (VSync)

options.info: Drive emulation timing from host vsync instead of the emulated console's refresh. Only takes effect when both VSync and Sync to Host Refresh Rate are enabled.
options.label: Use Host VSync Timing

options.info: Don't re-present a frame if the GS hasn't produced new output. Saves a tiny amount of GPU time. Mostly cosmetic in libretro mode.
options.label: Skip Presenting Duplicate Frames

options.info: How emulated audio is paced against host audio. TimeStretch resamples audio (via SoundTouch) so pitch stays correct when emulation speed differs from 100%. Disabled passes raw samples through — fastest but produces audible artefacts during any speed deviation.
options.label: Audio Sync Mode

options.info: Ring-buffer size for emulated audio in milliseconds. Smaller values reduce audio latency at the cost of higher CPU pressure and a greater chance of underruns (crackling). 50 ms is the PCSX2 default; 30 ms is a reasonable low-latency target on a machine that can sustain it.
options.label: Audio Buffer Size

options.info: Normal-play audio volume as a percentage. 100% is the PS2's native output level. Values above 100% boost the signal digitally (may clip on loud passages).
options.label: Volume

options.info: Volume during fast-forward as a percentage. Independent from the normal-play volume — useful for muting audio entirely during fast-forward without affecting regular playback.
options.label: Fast-Forward Volume

options.info: Mute all PS2 audio output. RetroNest's UI sounds and any other non-PS2 audio sources are unaffected.
options.label: Mute Audio

options.info: Inserts a virtual memory card into Slot 1. The card image is stored as Mcd001.ps2 under the per-game memcards folder. Disabling this prevents games from saving/loading via Slot 1.
options.label: Memory Card Slot 1

options.info: Inserts a virtual memory card into Slot 2. The card image is stored as Mcd002.ps2 under the per-game memcards folder. PCSX2 only auto-creates the file the first time a game writes to Slot 2.
options.label: Memory Card Slot 2

options.info: Enables the second memory-card slot of Multitap 1. Only useful when a game supports Multitap 1 and you need additional save slots for extra players.
options.label: Multitap 1 - Slot 2

options.info: Enables the third memory-card slot of Multitap 1.
options.label: Multitap 1 - Slot 3

options.info: Enables the fourth memory-card slot of Multitap 1.
options.label: Multitap 1 - Slot 4

options.info: Controls the aspect ratio of the emulated display. Auto picks 4:3 for interlaced games and 3:2 for progressive games. 16:9 stretches the image for widescreen TVs; Stretch fills the whole window.
options.label: Aspect Ratio

options.info: Overrides the aspect ratio only while full-motion video (FMV) is playing. Useful for games with widescreen cutscenes inside a 4:3 main game.
options.label: FMV Aspect Ratio Override

options.info: Selects how interlaced frames are combined for progressive display. Automatic picks the best option per game; Weave preserves detail; Bob and Blend smooth motion at the cost of vertical resolution.
options.label: Deinterlacing

options.info: Applies a bilinear filter when scaling the final image to the window. Smooth is the standard option; Sharp uses a pixel-art-friendly variant that keeps edges crisp.
options.label: Bilinear Filtering

options.info: Multiplies the display height after aspect-ratio fitting. Values above 100% make the image taller than its letterbox; values below leave extra vertical space. Standalone PCSX2 exposes a 10-300% slider; libretro offers enumerated stops.
options.label: Vertical Stretch

options.info: Trims pixels from the left edge of the source image before it's fit to the display window. Useful for games with garbage pixels at the border.
options.label: Crop - Left

options.info: Trims pixels from the top edge of the source image before it's fit to the display window.
options.label: Crop - Top

options.info: Trims pixels from the right edge of the source image before it's fit to the display window.
options.label: Crop - Right

options.info: Trims pixels from the bottom edge of the source image before it's fit to the display window.
options.label: Crop - Bottom

options.info: Automatically applies community widescreen patches to supported games. Reshapes the rendering to true 16:9 instead of stretching the 4:3 picture.
options.label: Apply Widescreen Patches

options.info: Automatically applies community no-interlacing patches to supported games. Removes flicker in games that render in interlaced mode.
options.label: Apply No-Interlacing Patches

options.info: Enables internal anti-blur hacks that remove the PS2's GS smear on commonly-affected games. Safe to leave on.
options.label: Anti-Blur

options.info: Snaps the rendered image to an integer multiple of the source pixel size. Produces crisp pixel-art scaling at the cost of leaving letterbox bars.
options.label: Integer Scaling

options.info: Enables PCRTC offsets so the screen is positioned exactly where the game requests. Fixes games that deliberately offset the viewport.
options.label: Screen Offsets

options.info: Disables the half-pixel interlace offset which can reduce jitter on some games that render at half vertical resolution.
options.label: Disable Interlace Offset

options.info: Shows the overscan area of the display that would normally be hidden by a CRT bezel. Exposes any garbage the game draws outside the safe area.
options.label: Show Overscan

options.info: Sets the internal rendering resolution. Higher values produce sharper visuals at the cost of GPU performance.
options.label: Internal Resolution

options.info: Controls how textures are sampled when rendered. Bilinear (PS2) matches the original hardware behavior; Forced options ignore the game's preference.
options.label: Texture Filtering

options.info: Enables trilinear filtering for smoother transitions between mipmap levels. Auto leaves this decision to each game.
options.label: Trilinear Filtering

options.info: Improves texture clarity at oblique viewing angles. Low cost on modern GPUs and generally safe to raise.
options.label: Anisotropic Filtering

options.info: Controls how PS2 dithering patterns are applied to upscaled rendering. Unscaled matches the original appearance.
options.label: Dithering

options.info: Controls how accurately PS2 blending operations are emulated. Higher levels improve compatibility with heavy effects at a performance cost.
options.label: Blending Accuracy

options.info: Emulates PS2 mipmapping when the hardware renderer is active. Fixes texture aliasing at distance in supported games.
options.label: Hardware Mipmapping

options.info: Loads replacement textures from the texture-replacement folder (per-game subdirectory keyed by disc serial). Only effective with a hardware renderer.
options.label: Load Textures

options.info: Dumps the game's textures to disk so they can be edited and loaded back as replacements. Writes to the per-game texture-replacement subdirectory. Only effective with a hardware renderer.
options.label: Dump Textures

options.info: Loads replacement textures on a background thread to avoid stutter at first sight of each texture. Disable only for deterministic capture or debugging.
options.label: Asynchronous Texture Loading

options.info: Loads every replacement texture for the current game at boot instead of on-demand. Uses more memory but eliminates load stutter mid-game.
options.label: Precache Replacements

options.info: When dumping textures, also writes each mipmap level. Useful for replacing distance LODs explicitly. Only meaningful with Dump Textures enabled.
options.label: Dump Mipmaps

options.info: Includes textures used during full-motion video in dumps. Off by default because FMV frames produce a large volume of one-off textures.
options.label: Dump Textures During FMV

options.info: AMD's Contrast Adaptive Sharpening pass on the final image. Sharpen Only sharpens at the internal render resolution; Sharpen and Resize sharpens at the display resolution.
options.label: Contrast Adaptive Sharpening (CAS)

options.info: Strength of the CAS sharpening pass. Higher values produce a sharper image with more visible noise. Standalone PCSX2 exposes a 1–100% slider; libretro offers enumerated stops.
options.label: CAS Sharpness

options.info: Fast Approximate Anti-Aliasing. A single-pass shader that softens jagged edges with low GPU cost.
options.label: FXAA

options.info: Applies a CRT-style filter to the final output for an authentic retro look. None disables the filter.
options.label: TV Shader

options.info: Master toggle for manual brightness, contrast, saturation, and gamma adjustment via the Shade Boost shader.
options.label: Shade Boost

options.info: Brightness multiplier when Shade Boost is enabled. 50% is neutral (no change); 0% blacks out the image; 100% is double brightness. Standalone PCSX2 exposes a 1–100% slider; libretro offers enumerated stops.
options.label: Shade Boost — Brightness

options.info: Contrast multiplier when Shade Boost is enabled. 50% is neutral (no change). Standalone PCSX2 exposes a 1–100% slider; libretro offers enumerated stops.
options.label: Shade Boost — Contrast

options.info: Color-saturation multiplier when Shade Boost is enabled. 50% is neutral (no change); 0% produces grayscale. Standalone PCSX2 exposes a 1–100% slider; libretro offers enumerated stops.
options.label: Shade Boost — Saturation

options.info: Gamma-correction multiplier when Shade Boost is enabled. 50% is neutral (no change); lower values darken midtones, higher values brighten midtones. Standalone PCSX2 exposes a 1–100% slider; libretro offers enumerated stops.
options.label: Shade Boost — Gamma

options.info: Global multiplier applied to every OSD overlay. 100% matches standalone PCSX2's default size. Standalone exposes a 25-500% slider; libretro offers enumerated stops.
options.label: OSD Scale

options.info: Pixel offset between the OSD elements and the screen edge. Standalone exposes a 0-100px slider; libretro offers enumerated stops.
options.label: OSD Margin

options.info: Corner where transient messages (save-state loaded, shader reload, etc.) are drawn. Set to None to hide them entirely.
options.label: OSD Messages Position

options.info: Corner where the performance stats column (FPS/Speed/CPU/GPU/etc.) is drawn. Set to None to hide the column and grey out every Performance Stats / System Information toggle.
options.label: OSD Performance Position

options.info: Renders OSD text in bold. Easier to read on bright scenes.
options.label: OSD Text Style (Bold)

options.info: Displays the emulation speed as a percentage. Red below 95%, green above 105%.
options.label: Show Speed Percentages

options.info: Displays the current frame rate reported by the GS. Useful for spotting performance issues.
options.label: Show FPS

options.info: Displays vertical syncs per second — the PS2 display refresh reported by the GS.
options.label: Show VPS

options.info: Displays the PS2 internal render resolution and interlacing mode.
options.label: Show Resolution

options.info: Displays per-frame GS statistics: draw-call count, VRAM use, and a frame-time summary.
options.label: Show GS Statistics

options.info: Displays per-component CPU usage (EE, GS, VU).
options.label: Show CPU Usage

options.info: Displays GPU usage percentage and frame time in milliseconds.
options.label: Show GPU Usage

options.info: Displays icons for pause, fast-forward, slow-motion, and turbo modes in the top-right corner.
options.label: Show Status Indicators

options.info: Displays a rolling graph of recent frame times to visualise stutter.
options.label: Show Frame Times

options.info: Displays the CPU and GPU model names as two lines in the performance column.
options.label: Show Hardware Info

options.info: Displays the PCSX2 version string in the performance column.
options.label: Show PCSX2 Version

options.info: Displays a compact summary of active emulation settings in the bottom-right corner.
options.label: Show Settings

options.info: Appends active patches (widescreen, no-interlacing, etc.) to the settings line. Requires Show Settings to be enabled.
options.label: Show Patches

options.info: Displays the current controller input state at the bottom-left corner.
options.label: Show Inputs

options.info: Displays a recording indicator while video capture is active. (Inert in the libretro variant — no FFmpeg capture is driven from this build.)
options.label: Show Video Capture Status

options.info: Displays an indicator while input recording is active. (Inert in the libretro variant — input recording UI is not driven from this build.)
options.label: Show Input Recording Status

options.info: Displays an indicator when replacement textures are loaded for the current game. Requires Texture Replacement → Load Textures to be enabled.
options.label: Show Texture Replacement Status

options.info: Shows a startup warning if any unsafe settings are enabled.
options.label: Warn About Unsafe Settings
